courses.course_name: Bachelor of Engineering in Agricultural Mechanization Engineering

salary: Ghana: GHS 36,000 - 84,000 annually (entry to mid-career, estimated)
mode: Full-time on-campus lectures and laboratory practicals
Field-based practicals and industrial attachments
entry_requirements: WASSCE with at least credits (A1-C6) in Core Mathematics, English Language, and Integrated Science
Credits in three relevant elective subjects including Physics, Mathematics and either Chemistry or Technical Drawing/Agricultural Science
Overall aggregate not exceeding 36 (estimated)
Mature and HND applicants may be considered for appropriate levels
level_note: Programme focuses on the design, operation and maintenance of agricultural machinery.
Students gain strong practical skills through field work and industrial attachments.

courses.course_name: Bachelor of Engineering in Automotive Engineering

mode: Full-time lectures combined with workshop and lab practicals
Industrial attachment in automotive garages and assembly plants
entry_requirements: WASSCE with at least credits (A1-C6) in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in three relevant electives including Physics and either Elective Mathematics, Technical Drawing, Auto Mechanics or related technical subjects
Competitive aggregate not exceeding 32 (estimated)
HND/technical diploma holders in automotive-related fields may gain advanced standing
level_note: Students are trained for the automotive industry with strong emphasis on diagnostics and maintenance.
The curriculum integrates theory with extensive workshop-based learning.

courses.course_name: Bachelor of Engineering in Chemical Engineering

mode: Full-time lectures, laboratory sessions and design projects
Industrial attachments in chemical and process industries
entry_requirements: WASSCE with credits (A1-C6) in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in three relevant elective subjects including Chemistry, Physics and Elective Mathematics
Competitive aggregate not exceeding 30 (estimated)
HND in Chemical/Process Engineering may qualify for top-up or level 200/300 entry
level_note: The programme equips students for roles in process industries such as food, petrochemicals, and manufacturing.
Strong emphasis is placed on safety, environmental sustainability and plant design.

courses.course_name: Bachelor of Engineering in Civil Engineering

mode: Full-time classroom teaching with design studios and lab work
Field trips and mandatory industrial attachments
entry_requirements: WASSCE with credits (A1-C6) in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in three relevant electives including Physics and Elective Mathematics plus one of Chemistry, Technical Drawing or Building Construction
Overall aggregate not exceeding 30 (estimated)
HND Civil or related diplomas may gain advanced placement
level_note: Students develop competencies in planning, designing and supervising civil engineering projects.

courses.course_name: Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Applied Statistics

Graduates are prepared for senior research and academic roles in universities, government and industry.

courses.course_name: Doctor of Technology in Automotive Engineering

courses.course_name: Doctor of Technology in Chemical Engineering

Detailed research proposal aligned with departmental focus areas

courses.course_name: Doctor of Technology in Civil Engineering

courses.course_name: Master of Arts (MA) in Marketing Management

Candidates from related disciplines may take bridging courses
level_note: Programme focuses on planning, design and management of roads and transport systems.
Graduates are equipped to handle complex highway and traffic engineering challenges.

courses.course_name: Master of Engineering In Civil Engineering (Structural Engineering)

mode: Coursework, design studio and computer-based modelling
Project or thesis on structural engineering topics
Evening/weekend or modular delivery for professionals (where applicable)
entry_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering or closely related area with at least Second Class Lower
Structural engineering background is an advantage
Interview and portfolio of design/engineering work may be requested
Bridging modules may be required for non-civil backgrounds
level_note: Programme deepens knowledge in analysis and design of complex structures.
Graduates can lead structural design teams and consultancies.

courses.course_name: Master of Engineering In Civil Engineering (Water and Environmental Engineering) (1 Year)
career: Policy and Planning Officer (Water and Environment)

mode: Intensive coursework with project-based learning
Field visits and case studies in water and sanitation projects
Short research project or professional project report
entry_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Civil, Environmental, Water Resources or related Engineering discipline
Good honours degree (normally Second Class Lower or better)
Professional experience in water or environmental sectors is an advantage
Selection may include interview and review of professional experience
level_note: Programme targets practising engineers requiring specialised water and environmental skills.
It is delivered in a compact format suitable for professionals.

courses.course_name: Master of Science in Agribusiness And Food Industry Management

electives: Policy and Institutional Frameworks in Agribusiness
mode: Coursework with case studies and industry projects
Weekend/evening modes available for professionals (varies)
Research project or thesis in final stage
entry_requirements: Good first degree in Agribusiness, Agriculture, Food Science, Economics, Business Administration or related field
Relevant work experience in agribusiness or food industry is an advantage
Applicants may attend an interview and present a statement of purpose
Other degree holders with significant agribusiness experience may be considered
level_note: Programme develops managerial and analytical competencies for the agrifood sector.
Graduates are prepared for leadership roles along agribusiness value chains.

courses.course_name: Master of Science in Banking and Finance (1 Year)

mode: Intensive coursework with case study and simulation focus
Weekend/evening or block teaching for professionals
Research project or applied finance project
entry_requirements: Good first degree in Banking and Finance, Accounting, Economics, Business Administration or related area
Relevant work experience in banking or finance preferred
Interview and review of academic transcripts may be required
Applicants from other backgrounds may take bridging courses in finance
level_note: Programme equips graduates for advanced roles in the banking and financial services sector.
It is structured to serve working professionals seeking rapid progression.

courses.course_name: Master of Science in Business Administration and Innovation (Accountancy)

mode: Coursework with case studies and group projects
Evening/weekend delivery to suit working professionals
Research or consultancy project in accounting and innovation
entry_requirements: Good first degree in Accounting or related field from a recognised institution
Professional accounting qualification (e.g. ICA, ACCA, CIMA) is an advantage
Relevant accounting or finance work experience preferred
Interview and assessment of academic/professional background
level_note: Programme combines advanced accounting with innovation and business transformation skills.
Graduates are prepared for senior finance roles in dynamic business environments.

courses.course_name: Master of Science in Business Administration and Innovation (Banking and Finance)

mode: Evening and weekend sessions for professionals
Case-based teaching and simulations
Applied research or consultancy project in banking and finance
entry_requirements: Good first degree in Banking and Finance, Accounting, Economics or related area
Relevant professional experience in banking or finance preferred
Interview and review of academic/professional record
Applicants from other fields may require bridging modules
level_note: Programme focuses on innovation and strategy within the banking and finance sector.
Graduates are equipped to drive change and new product development in financial institutions.

courses.course_name: Master of Science in Business Administration and Innovation (Logistics and Supply Chain Management)

mode: Weekend/evening classes for working professionals
Case studies and simulation-based learning
Research or capstone project in logistics and supply chain
entry_requirements: Good first degree in Logistics, Supply Chain, Procurement, Operations Management, Business Administration or related area
Relevant professional experience is an advantage
Interview and evaluation of academic/professional experience
Bridging modules may be needed for unrelated degree holders
level_note: Programme emphasises strategic and innovative approaches to supply chain management.
It prepares graduates for leadership roles in logistics and operations across sectors.

courses.course_name: Master of Science in Business Administration and Innovation (Management Organizational Development)

mode: Coursework with strong case and project components
Flexible delivery for working professionals
Research or consulting project on organisational development
entry_requirements: Good first degree in Management, HR, Business Administration or related social science field
Relevant managerial or HR experience is an advantage
Interview and review of academic/professional profile
Applicants from other fields may need preparatory management courses
level_note: Programme focuses on improving organisational effectiveness and managing change.

courses.course_name: Master of Science in Business Administration and Innovation (Procurement and Supply Chain Management)

mode: Evening/weekend teaching for professionals
Case-based and simulation-based learning
Research or practice-based project in procurement and supply chain
entry_requirements: Good first degree in Procurement, Supply Chain, Logistics, Business Administration or related area
Relevant professional experience in procurement or supply chain preferred
Interview and evaluation of academic/professional record
Professional procurement certifications are an advantage
level_note: Programme blends procurement strategy with innovative supply chain practices.

courses.course_name: Master of Science in Business Administration and Innovation (Strategic Marketing)

mode: Coursework with case-based teaching and projects
Flexible modes for working professionals
Strategic marketing project or thesis

Marketing or commercial work experience is an advantage
Interview and review of academic/professional profile

courses.course_name: Master of Science in Hospitality and Catering Management

Flexible scheduling for working professionals
Research or consultancy project in hospitality and tourism

courses.course_name: Higher Diploma In Health Statistics

mode: Full-time lectures and computer lab sessions
Practicum in health facilities and projects
entry_requirements: WASSCE with passes in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
At least three passes in relevant elective subjects such as Biology, Elective Mathematics or Economics
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Mature applicants with relevant experience may be considered
level_note: Programme prepares middle-level personnel for data roles in the health sector.
Graduates can progress to degree programmes in statistics or health information management.

courses.course_name: Higher National Diploma (HND) in Chemical Engineering

mode: Full-time lectures and laboratory practicals
Industrial attachment in chemical and process plants
entry_requirements: WASSCE with passes including at least credit in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits/passes in Chemistry and other science/technical electives
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Technical/vocational certificate holders may be considered
level_note: Programme provides practical skills for process and chemical industries.
Graduates may progress to BTech or BEng Chemical Engineering with credit transfer.

courses.course_name: Higher National Diploma (HND) In Dispensing Technology

mode: Full-time classroom teaching and practicals in simulated pharmacies
Clinical attachments in hospital and community pharmacies
entry_requirements: WASSCE with passes including credit in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits in science electives such as Chemistry and Biology
Aggregate generally not exceeding 42 (estimated)
Shortlisted applicants may be interviewed
level_note: Programme prepares students to support pharmacists in medicine dispensing and counselling.
Graduates can work in different pharmacy settings and progress to BTech Pharmaceutical Sciences.

courses.course_name: Higher National Diploma in Electrical and Electronic Engineering

mode: Full-time lectures and laboratory/workshop practicals
Industrial attachment in electrical and manufacturing firms
entry_requirements: WASSCE with passes including credit in English Language, Core Mathematics and Integrated Science
Credits/passes in Physics and other relevant technical/science electives

level_note: Programme equips students with hands-on skills for electrical and electronic maintenance roles.

courses.course_name: HND Accountancy

mode: Full-time lectures and tutorials with computer lab sessions
Industrial attachment in accounting and finance departments
entry_requirements: WASSCE with passes including credit in English Language, Core Mathematics and either Integrated Science or Social Studies
